comments.text: what is the importance of $a_n/b_n\to 1$ again? like why can this not hold for arbitrary infinite sets $A,B$?

For example let $A$ be the set of all integers with only even binary digits and $B$ the set of all integers with only odd binary digits. This construction also comes up in the closely related [331] (which I should link to this one). (The site has been updated to address this comment.)
Isn't there a problem in the statement, as we can never have $1 \in A + B$?
It should be just be that all large integers are in A+B (or allow 0). (The site has been updated to address this comment.)
